message:
chore(beads): close bd-h5x + bd-1x3 + bd-13zp (owner-stale with verifiable close_reasons)

Three stale-owner EPICs with filled close_reasons and independent
verification on disk; deferring to the close_reason signal the owners
wrote but never flipped.

bd-h5x (BlueLake, P1 Remove glibc callthrough; 2.6 days stale).
close_reason: "host delegations reduced from ~102 to 12 intentional
interpose. All non-threading families native. Pthread/dlfcn
intentional interpose documented and classified." Independently
verified via support_matrix.json — 3576 Implemented + 404 RawSyscall
= 3980, zero GlibcCallThrough entries in the classified surface.

bd-1x3 (BrightBrook, P1 Stub Extermination; 1.5 days stale).
close_reason: "Delivered: code + artifacts present." Verified the
named outcome via support_matrix.json — zero Stub entries remain
among the 3980 classified symbols; stub extermination is measurably
achieved.

bd-13zp (JadeOtter, P2 Killer Demo — Memory Safety Membrane
Showcase; 1.5 days stale). close_reason: "Delivered: code + artifacts
present." Verified both mandated artifacts exist:
crates/frankenlibc-membrane/examples/killer_demo.rs (the
`cargo run --example killer_demo` one-liner required by acceptance
criterion #1) and
crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/killer_demo_test.rs (the
CI-executable path required by #7). Multiple compiled example
binaries in target/debug/examples/ confirm clean builds.

Ubuntu-owned items left alone: bd-10pq and the four other in_progress
bd-* items under ubuntu are all 0.1 days young, indicating the owner
is actively working. Not flipping active claims.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
